headline: Jim Cramer on Costco: “Never Ever Cheap, and It Is Always Terrific”
summary: Costco Wholesale Corporation (NASDAQ:COST) was among Jim Cramer’s latest stock calls, as he advised investors on how to manage data center-related stock positions. A caller asked for Cramer’s current thoughts on its “valuation and growth opportunities,” and Cramer replied: Okay, Costco sells at 49 times earnings. That’s where it’s been, 47, 49 for the last […]

headline: Purple Innovation Q1 Earnings Call Highlights

headline: Costco vs. Dollar General: Which Discount Retailer Holds More Promise?
summary: Costco looks more promising than Dollar General as membership fees jump, Kirkland expands, digital sales grow, and 28 new warehouses are planned for fiscal 2026.

headline: Costco vs. Target: One Stock Is Near Its All-Time High — The Other Is in Freefall
summary: Costco (NASDAQ:COST) and Target (NYSE:TGT) just delivered earnings that crystallize the gap between premium membership retail and traditional discretionary discount. Costco posted another beat with comparable traffic still climbing. Target, under its newly seated CEO Michael Fiddelke, eked out a bottom-line beat on shrinking sales. Same sector, same shopper, very different operating realities. Membership Flywheel ... Costco vs. Target: One Stock Is Near Its All-Time High — The Other Is in Freefa

headline: We started a burrito spot in a Home Depot parking lot – it brings in $2.3 million/year
summary: Lydia Holmes and John Clarke started a food shed in a Home Depot parking lot called LJ's Lil' Café. Business was slow at first, but after Eater LA published a rave review of their breakfast burritos, their sales skyrocketed. In 2025, Holmes and Clarke opened a second location and brought in over $2.3 million in sales for the year.
